name: Agent: Landing Page A/B Testing
summary: Tests the landing page over and over so it keeps learning what works — and stops to ask a person at the points where judgment beats a rule.
objectives: Keep testing the landing page, non-stop, so it keeps learning what works.
Learn two things separately: what brings leads, and what brings sales.
A person decides the idea, the creative and the winner. The agent does everything in between.
When a message wins, use it beyond the page that proved it.

outcomes.text: The whole loop is written down: eight steps from idea to reusing the winner, with the points marked where a person decides. Someone who did not build it can run it.
It was walked end to end on a practice run before being pointed at anything real: every document produced, every approval enforced, and nothing sent outside.

Meeting the real testing tool changed the plan — and the plan was corrected, rather than the reality. Publishing a test by hand is now a normal path with its own instructions, instead of the case the agent had assumed away.

The rule for picking a winner now follows the math the testing tool actually reports, instead of a number the plan had borrowed from a different kind of test. That number would have looked official and meant nothing.

milestones.title: Write the eight-step loop, from idea to reusing the winner
Put every setting in a file per deployment, so the agent stays portable
Build the dashboard where a person approves the idea, the creative and the winner
Walk the whole loop on a practice run
Write the instructions for publishing and reading a test by hand
Publish the guide, so someone who did not build it can run it
Get access to the analytics account that reports the results — what the project is waiting on, and the last piece before a real test can run
Run the first real test on a live page
